'''Jansin Turgut''' (born 8 March 1996) is a [[Turkey national rugby league team|Turkish]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a back row forward. He previously played for [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]. Turgut captained the [[Turkey national rugby league team|Turkish national team]] during their international debut at the [[2018 Emerging Nations World Championship]].

==Background==
Turgut was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England,<ref name=profile /> and is of [[Turkish people|Turkish]] descent through his father.<ref name=enwc>{{cite web|last=Clarke|first=Paul|date=22 August 2018|title=Jansin Turgut discusses his Turkish delight after landing Emerging Nations call-up|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/jansin-turgut-turkey-rugby-league-1923111|publisher=Hull Daily Mail|accessdate=23 August 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Turgut represented England Academy (u18) against the [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] in 2014.<ref name=enwc />

===Hull===
Turgut made his Hull F.C. début on 5 March 2015 in a Super League match against [[Leeds Rhinos]] at the [[KC Stadium]].

===Doncaster===
In 2015, Turgut was dual registered with [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] club [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]. He made his Dons début on 15 March 2015 in a match against [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]].

===Salford===
Turgut joined [[Salford Red Devils]] "on a short-term deal" towards the end of the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|date=3 August 2018|title=Jansin Turgut: Salford Red Devils sign Hull FC outside-back|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/45062898|publisher=BBC|accessdate=23 August 2018}}</ref>
==Coaching==
On 17 June 2020 it was announced that he had joined the coaching staff of [[West Hull A.R.L.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=17 Jun 2020|accessdate=26 Jan 2021|title=Jansin Turgut makes return to rugby league after landing coaching role|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jansin-turgut-west-hull-return-4234908}}</ref>

==Personal==
On 21 May 2019, it was reported that Turgut had fallen three storeys; attaining severe injuries and being put on life support.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://news.sky.com/story/jansin-turgut-rugby-league-player-on-life-support-in-ibiza-after-three-storey-car-park-fall-11725782 | title=Jansin Turgut: Rugby league player on life support in Ibiza after three-storey car park fall | date=21 May 2019 | publisher=Sky News | accessdate=24 January 2020 }}</ref>

On 25 January 2020 he admitted that the fall from a third storey car park in Ibiza had been a suicide attempt.<ref>{{cite web|website=Hull Daily Mail|date=25 Jan 2020|accessdate=26 Jan 2021|title=Former Hull FC player Jansin Turgut admits Ibiza car park fall was an attempt to take his life|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-news/jansin-turgut-ibiza-fall-life-3777204}}</ref>
